name: Approve or reject new Dokan marketplace vendors from Telegram

parameters.content:
## Dokan — Vendor Approval Workflow

### How it works

1. A schedule trigger polls your Dokan site every 15 minutes for vendors awaiting approval.
2. New pending vendors are de-duplicated and announced to Telegram with Approve, Reject and View profile buttons.
3. Clicking Approve calls a token-protected webhook that sets the vendor to `active` in Dokan.
4. Clicking Reject sets the vendor to `inactive`, keeping the record recoverable.
5. Dokan sends the welcome or rejection email natively, and the workflow posts a confirmation back to Telegram.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Replace `your-marketplace.com` with your Dokan site URL (HTTP Request nodes + View profile button).
- [ ] Replace `your-n8n-instance.com` with your n8n public URL (Approve/Reject buttons).
- [ ] Set `YOUR_APPROVAL_TOKEN` to a long random string guarding the approve/reject webhooks.
- [ ] Set `YOUR_TELEGRAM_CHANNEL_ID` to your target Telegram channel ID.
- [ ] Add the `Dokan Application Password` credential (Basic Auth, user with `manage_woocommerce`).
- [ ] Add the `Telegram Bot Token` credential (bot with `chat:write`).

### Customization

Vendor welcome and rejection emails are sent by Dokan — configure templates under wp-admin → Dokan → Settings → Email Settings. The "Skip already-announced" node uses n8n static data to avoid re-posting; the approve/reject branches clear it, so a vendor toggled back to pending is re-announced. Pair with the New Vendor Onboarding template to extend past approval.

## Poll & announce new vendors

Every 15 minutes, fetch pending Dokan vendors over the REST API, drop any already posted (dedupe via static data), then announce each one to Telegram with Approve, Reject and View profile buttons.

## Approve branch

Admin clicks Approve, firing a token-checked webhook that sets the vendor status to `active` in Dokan, clears the dedupe flag and posts a confirmation to Telegram. Dokan sends the welcome email natively on status change.

## Reject branch

Admin clicks Reject, firing a token-checked webhook that sets the vendor status to `inactive` (record kept, recoverable), clears the dedupe flag and posts a confirmation to Telegram. Dokan sends the rejection email natively on status change.

nodes.name: Every 15 minutes
nodes.notes: Polls every 15 min. Replace with a real-time trigger once Dokan exposes dokan_new_seller_created as a WooCommerce webhook topic (see README → Real-time variants).

nodes.name: Fetch pending vendors
nodes.notes: status=inactive returns vendors awaiting approval. Requires admin caps (manage_woocommerce).

nodes.name: Skip already-announced
nodes.notes: n8n static data is per-workflow and survives executions. The dedupe entry is cleared by the approve/reject branches so the workflow self-heals if a vendor toggles back to pending.

nodes.name: Confirm approval (Telegram)
nodes.notes: Posts new approval confirmation message to Telegram. Mirrors the Slack version's behavior (new message, not edit-in-place).

nodes.name: Mark inactive
nodes.notes: Sets to inactive rather than DELETE so the vendor record (and audit trail) is preserved. If you'd rather hard-delete, swap this for DELETE /dokan/v1/stores/{id}.

nodes.name: Confirm rejection (Telegram)
nodes.notes: Posts new rejection confirmation message to Telegram. Mirrors the Slack version's behavior.

nodes.name: Post to Telegram channel
nodes.notes: Posts approval request to Telegram channel. Replace YOUR_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID with your channel's -100... ID. Bot token is hardcoded in the URL. Buttons use the same n8n webhooks as the Slack version; behavior is identical from the user perspective.

nodes.name: Respond: approved
nodes.notes: HTML response with auto-close attempt. Best-effort: closes ~80% of browser sessions; fallback message appears if browser blocks the close.

parameters.responseBody:
✅

Approved

Closing this tab…

nodes.name: Respond: rejected
nodes.notes: HTML response with auto-close attempt. Best-effort: closes ~80% of browser sessions; fallback message appears if browser blocks the close.

parameters.responseBody:
❌

Rejected

Closing this tab…
